methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Finwave Semiconductor is a Series A chip design company founded by Tomas Palacios that has raised $25M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private peers (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) valued between $500M and $5.0B, alongside a public semiconductor cohort (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 22.3x.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 2.0x multiplier to the $25M raised proxy, implies a $50M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields a $1.5B median valuation, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue data, common among private companies. The wide dispersion in peer valuations reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and investor appetite within frontier semiconductor ventures.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $42M (floor, stage-progression basis) to $1.7B (ceiling, peer-median plus upside), with a median of $775M. The substantial gap between methods reflects fundamental uncertainty around Finwave's technical differentiation relative to better-capitalized peers and path to revenue. Resolution of IP strength, customer traction, and manufacturing partnerships would materially narrow this range.
